description: Extracts PO numbers and full appointment details (appointment_number, client, consignee, appointment_date_time, c3_response, po_numbers) from Monday.com C3 board updates. Supports Sobeys and Loblaw email formats. Use start_extract_c3_appointment + check_job_status for long-running runs to avoid timeouts.

capabilities.name: extract_c3_po_numbers
capabilities.description: Fetches the Updates (email body) for the given item_id(s), parses any table that has a 'Purchase Order Number' column, and returns all PO numbers per item.

item_ids.description: Multiple item IDs for batch results.

capabilities.name: start_extract_c3_appointment
capabilities.description: RECOMMENDED for agentic platforms: Starts C3 appointment extraction in the background and returns immediately with a job_id. Does not require board_id or group_name (defaults are used). Poll check_job_status with the returned job_id every 10-30 seconds until status is completed or failed. Use this instead of extract_c3_appointment_details to avoid timeouts.

limit.description: Max rows to process. Default 5. Use 2 or 5 when hosting.

config_path.description: Path to Altruos config JSON. Omit to skip Shipment/Load ID lookups.

capabilities.name: check_job_status
capabilities.description: Check the status of a background extract job started with start_extract_c3_appointment. Returns status (pending/running/completed/failed), progress, and when completed the final_result (items, count, saved_path). Poll after calling start_extract_c3_appointment until status is completed or failed.

capabilities.name: extract_c3_appointment_details
capabilities.description: SYNCHRONOUS: Fetches first N rows from C3 board where Row Type is empty; extracts appointment_number, client, consignee, appointment_date_time, c3_response, po_numbers. board_id and group_name are optional (defaults used). May timeout on platforms; prefer start_extract_c3_appointment + check_job_status for long runs.

config_path.description: Path to Altruos config. Omit to skip Shipment/Load ID lookups.

author: neehar
